title: Amazfit Zepp E Circle Men's Smart Watch for Android Phone iPhone Health Fitness Tracker, 5 ATM Waterproof, Always On Display, Heart Rate, Blood Oxygen, Sleep, Stress Monitoring, Polar Night Black
description: Amazfit Zepp E Circle Men's Smart Watch For Android Phone iPhone Health Fitness Tracker, 5 ATM Waterproof, Always On Display, with Heart Rate, Blood Oxygen, Sleep, Stress Monitoring, Polar Night Black.
features: ULTRA-SLIM METAL BODY WITH 3D CURVED BEZEL-LESS DESIGN: Zepp E Circle is equipped with pure black 3D curved bezel-less glass and polished stainless-steel back perfectly fused together, making the watch sleek and ultra-slim. With only 9 mm thickness, it's exceptionally comfortable to wear day and night.

BLOOD OXYGEN SATURATION MEASUREMENT: SpO2 is an important indicator of health, especially in high-altitude and low-oxygen environments or while performing high-intensity sports. With Zepp E, you can measure your current blood-oxygen saturation instantly from your wrist.

SLEEP QUALITY MONITORING: Monitor light sleep, deep sleep, rapid eye movement (REM), and awake time. It also provides you a score on sleep quality and breathing quality. It even monitors your 20-minute nap during the day.
7-DAY BATTERY LIFE: Zepp E endures for up to 7 days in typical usages mode for a single full charge, which free you from the daily recharge.

NOTIFICATIONS AND ALARMS: You will also receive notifications when your heart rate exceeds the recommended limit. Zepp E Circle receives notifications for incoming calls, emails, messages and other applications without having the phone; sedentary reminders if you've been sitting too long.
Included components: User Manual
Connectivity technology: Bluetooth

reviews.title: Beautiful Smart Watch
reviews.text: Very pleased.  Handsome titanium case.  Looks more like a  traditional watch than a smart watch.  All functions work on my IPhone 13.  Great choice of faces, including several analog choices.  Always on display is great,  Only one semi-negative.  You must have patience during the pairing process.  Same thing true with programming.  But once it is done, could not ask more from a reasonably priced smart watch. No help from a basically useless manual.  You are on your own.  But, with patience, worth the time it takes to figure out how to pair and operate  Took me about an hour.  Highly recommended

reviews.title: Battery life runs out quickly.
reviews.text: Nice looking.  Great apps and tracking. But battery life isn't all that great.

reviews.title: A good start but needs a lot of work.
reviews.text:
This is a tough review to write. If I could do half stars, this watch would be a 3.5 for me. It does so much right but it feels incomplete and overpriced to me.

First off, the screen and overall build quality is amazing. I have a Galaxy Active 2 as well and, candidly, this Zepp watch is better in pretty much every way in terms of build. The glass feels better and more scratch resistant. You get a stainless frame instead of aluminum. The shocker is the screen. I am not sure how but this watch looks brighter and crisper to me. The size is really nice as well. The Zepp E has a lower profile and almost no visible bezel. If hardware was the lone consideration, this would get a near perfect score.

The battery life is also really outstanding. I get 5 to 7 days with this. It has a proprietary magnetic charger that works well. Even playing with it often and tracking regular workouts doesn't seem to bother it.

I also like Zepp's approach to overall health measurement. The PAI (Personal Activity Intelligence) score tries to give you an overall measurement of how healthy your days are. Once you understand it, I think it is a good general snap shot. If everyone tracked their life like this, the world would be a much healthier place. Sleep tracking is good as well. In line with what I have gotten from Fitbit and Samsung though you need to make sure to set a daily screen timer as it doesn't automatically know to turn off the screen when you are sleeping like the others.

From here though things begin to slip. At $250, there is no onboard GPS. This is a big omission at this price. There also isn't auto tracking for workouts like most others have so you have to remember to physically start tracking workouts. There is no onboard music player though you can control your music from the watch. Interacting with your notifications and inbound calls are all but worthless. Zepp's app is also a mess. It is cumbersome and not well thought out. Everything you want is there but it often takes alot of effort to get from one thing to another. Samsung's app is vastly superior. Lastly, the watch comes with a leather strap. At the time of this writing, Zepp has silicone straps listed on their site but they don't appear to be available yet. A watch that is focused on health should come default with a silicone strap.

I guess I am just not sure what Zepp set out to accomplish with this watch. Is it a smart watch? Not really. Is it a fitness watch? Kind of. Its like they tried to split the baby and ended up accomplishing neither successfully. If you want a good looking everyday digital semi-smart watch with a great battery life, this is worth considering but, for most, at $250, there are better options out there. Perhaps Zepp will update this product and make it more well rounded. If not, I would suggest waiting for a price reduction before investing.

reviews.title: Only thing missing is the talk and messaging
reviews.text: Otherwise, it is similar to Apple watch. I easily set this up and downloaded the software from the app store. It installed easily with absolutely zero issues and you know it is always the software that has glitches. This watch is probably one of the better health smart watches on the market.. It looks nice, it performs nice and has lots of features. You will love the battery life, especially if you have a smartwatch that has to be charged almost daily or maybe ever day and a half. The watch monitors sleep stages, heart rate and 11 different exercises, including swimming. And, it is nice looking. Pretty sweet. I wanted to add that you can select so many faces for your watch without extra charges.

reviews.title: Broke 3 weeks after purchasing
reviews.text: To be fair with the manufacturer, one does not expect for your watch to fall often. In this case, the only time it fell, BOOM - the screen broke on one corner and the display became unusable.

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price.
reviews.text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

reviews.title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
reviews.text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

reviews.title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
reviews.text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

reviews.title: Did not like this nearly as much as my original amazfit bip watch
reviews.text:
I bought an Amazfit Bip watch a year or more ago and have really been enjoying it The app tracks all my cycling trips and the sleep monitoring is useful. Iam really a big fan of the long battery life of the older model.

This watch has a 9 day battery life which is not nearly as long as the 30 days of the original watch.

This watch meausres blood oxygen levels but taking the measurement requires holding your wrist it the right position and takes much longer that the finger tip device Ihave.

I did not exhaustively evaluate the new app that came with this phone but it did not look nearly as good as the original app.

reviews.title: Better than my Apple Watch
reviews.text: Got this for the kiddo. He’s 14 and Captain Destructo. This kid literally body slams trees for fun and the best and sturdiest gifts for him last about a week. Two if you’re lucky. We’re never lucky. However, it’s been a while and he wears it nonstop. Get this, it still works. He loves the band and all of the features. The battery life makes my jaw drop. I have to charge my Apple Watch at least once a day. This watch does more and has a battery life that won’t quit. I’d fight him for it but again…he body slams trees. Great purchase, guys. It’s also a smart and inexpensive way to test your kid’s level of responsibility before buying more expensive products and flushing your money.

reviews.title: Good quality and not overpriced
reviews.text: I bought this product as present for my son, he enjoys wearing it. I like the quality. And it does not cost an arm and a leg.

reviews.title: Good one
reviews.text: Presented it to my cousin and he loved it alot.

reviews.title: Easy set up
reviews.text: Easy set up to any phone, and it offers the same features that the name brand smart watches offer for a fraction of the cost.  There are some differences with display appearances which is expected for the price, text come through like reading an old dot matrix system but it gets the job done.

title: SAMSUNG GEAR S3 FRONTIER Smartwatch 46MM (Bluetooth Only) - Dark Grey (Renewed)
description:
Stay in touch, without reaching for your phone, with the smartwatch that’s engineered for adventure. Convenience being the key feature, the watch offers fitness tracking, app access and watch face customization. With the Gear S3, you can pay right from your wrist and track your location as well. The Gear S3 is compatible with both Android and iOS smartphones.
Never Miss a Moment
The Gear S3 is Bluetooth connected so you can receive texts, emails, and alerts without reaching for your phone. With a built-in speaker and microphone, you can also make and answer calls hands-free. Just rotate the distinctive steel bezel to easily access apps and notifications so you can instantly check texts, updates, and more.
Pay with Your Watch
With Samsung Pay on your Gear S3, you can easily make payments with your watch almost anywhere you can swipe or tap a credit card - even without your phone or wallet.
Get Out There
Whether you’re off-roading or exploring a new city, the built-in GPS tracks your activity and makes it easy to share your location.
Designed for The Elements
The military-grade Gear S3 is tough enough to handle the elements. It resists water, dust, extreme temperatures, and the occasional drop so you can venture out with confidence.
Go for Days
Go nonstop for days on a single charge.4 When it’s time to recharge, the wireless charger lets you power up without slowing down.
Tailored to You
Make the Gear S3 your own. Customize your watch face, choosing from thousands of striking colors and designs. Plus easily swap out the standard 22mm band.
features: The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
Text, call and get notifications directly from your watch through your Bluetooth connected smartwatch.
Access the apps you use every day on your Gear S3. Catch a ride, check scores, stream music, and more.
With the built-in S Health app, track your steps, monitor your heart rate, and more.

reviews.title: Just over 90 days and it completely died
reviews.text: Rrally liked this watch at first. The battery did not last very long, even after adjusting settings. Thought it was the battery so we bought a new one, nope just completely died now. The new battery is fine, charges up and works, but the watch will not even turn on with new battery in it.

reviews.title: Love this
reviews.text: Love this watch

reviews.title: Husband LOVES This
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband for his birthday and Father's Day and he loves it. He used to have a Galaxy S9, but dropped it into a lake while we were fishing *face palm*. But he ended up getting an old S7 from a family member and the watch connects with that as well. The only complaint I have heard from him is about the band, which was easy and cheap to replace with a metal material that didn't stick to his wrist as much as the rubbery band that comes with the watch.

reviews.title: Great looking watch!
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband and he loves it!  He answers his phone and text messages right on the watch.  He doesn't have an excuse not to answer me now :D

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text:
I bought this when it first came out, I needed it's walking and stair climbing metrics.

It's big, heavy, and if you're at all active, it's on the charge cradle for around 1 hour. The big, heavy part, some like it, I wish it were a little smaller. The battery life, it's an early version, I didn't expect much, my next watch, 20 day minimum.

All in all, it's a very functional watch, feature laden, and good, as long as Samsung supports the OS

reviews.title: Seems to work pretty well for the most part
reviews.text: Setup was easy with a quick app download and it paired with my iPhone 12 without any drama. Biometric tracking seems to work reasonably well with the exception of heart rate, which seems to always read 70 no matter what I'm doing. I'm not too crazy about the aesthetic of the watch, it just looks kind of cheap to me. I got this mainly to use as a health tracking tool and to tell time, not to make and receive calls/texts with, so I haven't setup any of those features yet and may never get around to doing so. So i can't comment on ease of use for any of those features. I did like that there are quite a lot of different watch face designs that can be applied and without any additional purchase. For the price of around $40, this is a pretty smokin' deal for a smart watch, I think.

reviews.title: Not bad for the price
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a few different affordable watches and my biggest issue has been the charging port. They have trouble staying connected with tiny pins and no magnetic pull. So going into this I’m more critical.
Upside is there is definitely a better charging system. It holds in place nicely even though it’s a pretty small pin area it is easier to charge.
The screen does have low sensitivity response. When pairing it it took 3 tries to get it recognize I had tapped the screen.
On a good note it’s reading well and has some great features like heart rate, oxygen, and sleep. Everything lined up well with my normal statistics so I feel it’s pretty accurate.
There’s a variety of images to choose from so you can customize the screen however you would like.
It’s very comfortable. The band holds like a premium watch band. It has a large face that is classy. Aesthetically it’s very nice.
Overall for an affordable smart watch it’s a decent option.

reviews.title: Okay tracker
reviews.text: For the price, it is an okay tracker.  The microphone on the watch is not of great quality as I was told sounded muffled on it.  It does have a lot of features and was quite easy to use but nothing about it made it stand out from the other hundreds of smart watches out on the market.

reviews.title: Cute watch is all I can say
reviews.text: Initially the watch was quick to charge and holds the charge for a couple of days.  The functions were easy to use, and a lot to choose from for the activities I do.  I mainly track my steps, distance, duration, heart rate.  It worked well for a few weeks, then I took it swimming for a few laps in the pool.  That was a mistake, battery stopped working (won't charge anymore).  Sad, I really liked the watch, it's cute but the battery died.

reviews.title: Great Smart watch for the essentials
reviews.text:
I was looking for a good smart watch for my mother. She was a Fitbit user but her Fitbit died after years of use and wanted something with a similar feel.
This watch really worked out for her.
It's got a great screen and a good amount of apps to provide essential uses.
It's able to work with iOS and Android.
It's able to get notifications from apps and make phone calls.
Not a bad product at all.

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
Pretty good smartwatch. I needed something to replace my Galaxy Active, but didn't want to spend $100+ on something with a horrible battery life. I bought this one and for a while I was disappointed. I had to charge the watch each night and the steps weren't as accurate. I changed the setting so that the screen doesn't turn on when you move your wrist and that has saved the battery big time. I'm on day 4 and only down to 50% which means I'm now able to track sleep as well. I still think the steps aren't as accurate because it almost matches the steps in my phone's health app, but I'm notorious for leaving my phone places. I also miss being able to limit the options for exercise types in the watch face. I have to scroll through a long list each time. Overall, pretty good product for a cheaper price.

Update: I originally gave this 5 stars, but I have to take 2 away. A few days ago, the watch all of a sudden stopped counting steps. For the price, it was perfect until it stopped working. Back to the drawing board.

reviews.title: The perfect watch

reviews.title: Comfortable, worth the value
reviews.text: The band that originally came with the Fitbit was so uncomfortable. It dig into my wrist and left marks. I ordered another band that was about the same price as all four of these. It was useless. Barely fit, and it wouldn’t keep its position, so I decided to try these. First, I couldn’t believe that the same price got me FOUR bands in different colors! The bands are easily adjustable. Once fitted, these stay in place on the wrist. They’re comfy, and they stretch enough so that I can pull off the Fitbit to charge without having to readjust the band. Though inexpensive, this particular purchase was one of the best I’ve had this year, LOL.

reviews.title: Really love them but 2 already broke after 2 weeks
reviews.text: I really love these, I hate the bands that come with the fitbits. These are really comfortable but 1 band wouldn't work for some reason then another band broke after 2 weeks without even consistently wearing it.

reviews.title: Did NOT Work with Sense 2
reviews.text: Not sure what the issue is, but I could not get any of these bands to engage and lock with the Sense 2.  The tab fits into the slot just fine, but it won't engage the locking tab to hold the band into place.  Pulling on the band at all causes it to simply fall out of the watch case.  The bands themselves look fine, but not locking into place obviously makes them worthless for their intended purpose.

reviews.title: My Approach S6 -- Near Perfection!
reviews.text: Very pleased with the quality of this band replacement for my Approach S6 band and the ease of installation. Having a buddy helps, but the installation is easily accomplished on your own within a matter of minutes.  The new band is more flexible than the original which, IMHO, is a plus.  What I can't say is that the original band might have stiffened with age.  Who knows and it doesn't matter.  The screwdrivers are also an awesome design -- the handle ends have a hex shape you can stick in your drill if your hands are weak.  The only minuscule minus is that the retainer bar/strap is a tad large as others have stated, but problem solved by reusing the retainer strap from the original band. VERY pleased with this purchase.

reviews.title: DO NOT BUY worked less than 60 days and no customer support.
reviews.text:
DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. YOU WILL REGRET IT!!!!
UPDATE: Less than 60 days and the watched completely stopped working.
Being new to using a smart watch I didn't want to invest a fortune. Read lots of reviews and decided to give this one a try. It is perfect. It does everything I want it to do. It looks great. It is simple to set it up as you want. AND they provided exceptional customer service!

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text: This watch has never worked and the instructions did not help.

reviews.title: There are TWO ways of rating this... Garbage or almost garbage.
reviews.text:
There are two ways of rating purchases... either an overall evaluation IRREGARDLESS of the price OR... CONSIDERING THE PRICE. Considering I paid $20 for it... not horrible. BUT here are the SERIOUS flaws so far:

-The Blood Pressure monitor is a COMPLETE JOKE. "Inaccurate" is NOT the word. I tried it on several friends and coworkers. Garbage!
-You are supposed to be able to make a gesture of bringing your wrist up to view the watch and then it is supposed to turn the screen on so that you can read it. ALMOST NEVER! No matter HOW I make the gesture the only thing that works is a PAINFUL twist/shake of my wrist and then it's not guaranteed. I just use the button instead which kind of defeats the purpose.
-BUT THEN, when sleeping.... EVERY DAMNED MOVEMENT I MAKE it turns on... and waking up my wife, because it is like having a flashlight shone in your face!!!!!!!!!!!

So... EVEN THOUGH this was only $20... SAVE YOUR MONEY FOR SOMETHING THAT ACTUALLY WORKS AND WORKS WELL.

reviews.title: Great Smart Watch!
reviews.text: It is a great dupe of Apple Watch.  I absolutely love it. The greatest attraction is the extended battery time (7 to 10 days). I also like various fitness track features such as step count, heart-beat, workout routine. I could check weather too. Another interesting feature is the multiple watch face options. However I struggled a bit figuring out how to change those watch faces. The watch faces can be changed easily using the APP. I found the questions and answers mentioned on the webpage pretty helpful when figuring out other elements.

reviews.title: Bad watch!
reviews.text: The mobile app works fine! But after five months the watch failed to take a charge!! Try a new charger and the watch still wouldn't take a charge! Now I have a Dead watch! A watch should last longer than five months!!!!!

title: UMAXGET Leather Band Compatible with Fitbit Blaze Bands, Retro Cowhide Genuine Leather Replacement Band with Black or Silver Metal Frame Compatible with Fitbit Blaze Smart Watch for Men Women
features: 【 Applicatble Type 】- UMAXGET genuine leather band is only compatible with Fitbit Blaze Bands. Small size fits 5.9-7.4 inches (150mm-190mm) wrist; Large size fits 6.8-8.1 inches (175mm-225mm) wrist. Please choose the csize according to your wrist size.

【 Excellent Quality 】- Our compatible with Fitbit Blaze Bands for men is made of 100% genuine leather material, comfortable and skin-friendly. Every band is carefully and beautifully cut. Even the band edge is smooth to touch. Reinforced and exquisite stitching ensures practicality.

【 Unique Design 】- UMAXGET Compatible for fitbit blaze bands for women, there black and silver metal frame for you to choose, both can perfectly match your Fitbit Blaze smartwatch, makes your sporty fitness tracker look like a very classic watch.

【 Easy Installation 】- The metal frame is high quality and durable, come with 2 free pins, easy to install and remove, no tools needed, can be locked onto your Fitbit Blaze watch securely and tightly, fit seamlessly into your watch interface.

【 Wearing Tips 】- New leather band is a little stiff at first. It would soften up on your wrist after wearing it for several times. And please remember to dry it off when it gets wet. If you have any questions, please contact us in time, we will help you in the first time.

reviews.title: This band is really excellent quality. Fit is great
reviews.text:
This band is really excellent quality. Fit is great, leather is great. It is stiff (leather) at first, but that is like any leather product. It quickly adapts to your wrist and looks great. Their customer service is what I expect, top notch.
\

reviews.title: Looks good for the price and seems sturdy so far.
reviews.text: Looks good. Came flat and needed a few days to soften up. Not the nicest leather but this was a bargain FitBit band, not a Coach handbag. Very pleased.

reviews.title: Way too long
reviews.text: The length is way too long even though I ordered a small. Could not use it all had to send it back after putting it on my watch and trying it on and home. Too bad to because it looks really nice.

reviews.title: Very stiff
reviews.text: The buttons don’t work when it’s inside the band. A waste of money

reviews.title: Looks and Feels Great
reviews.text: Looks and Feels great, feels very comfortable.
